meta.description: Career pathway templates for the LabLink Initiative CDP gap analysis engine. Each pathway defines the skill requirements, recommended coursework, and target programs for a specific career track. Used to generate personalized gap analyses for students.
meta.howToUse: Each pathway has a 'skills' array with weight 1-5 (5 = most critical). The gap analysis compares student skills against this list. 'corePrograms' links to program IDs in programs.json.

pathways.name: DOE National Lab Research Internship
pathways.description: The foundation pathway for DOE national lab research internships (SULI/CCI). Covers the skills and preparation needed to compete for and succeed in a national laboratory research placement.
pathways.timeToReady: 6-18 months for students starting with science coursework; faster for students already in STEM programs

skills.name: Lab safety basics (OSHA/campus lab safety training)
Zintellect account created and profile complete
Personal statement / research interest statement drafted

recommendedCourses: Statistics for Scientists or Data Analysis
certifications: OSHA 10-hour General Industry (or campus lab safety equivalent)
Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) training (usually free online)
applicationTips: Apply to 5+ labs on Zintellect — the platform allows multiple simultaneous applications
SULI and CCI share the January deadline — submit both on one profile
Your research interest statement should mention specific lab capabilities (e.g., 'ORNL's Spallation Neutron Source')
Weak GPA? Address it proactively in your personal statement — show trajectory
Community college students: CCI is LESS competitive than SULI — higher acceptance rate
Reach out to your local PTK chapter — LLI has direct connections through our PTK partnership
lliFreeResources: LLI Free Guide: 'Your Path to a National Lab Internship' — download at lablinkinitiative.org
LLI Webinar recording: PTK × INL × LLI session on SULI/CCI strategy
LinkBot Newsletter — monthly deep-tech topics from the labs you're applying to

pathways.name: Nuclear Engineering & Science
pathways.description: Pathway for students pursuing nuclear engineering, nuclear science, or nuclear security careers at DOE national labs and the nuclear industry.
pathways.timeToReady: 12-24 months for undergrads with physics/math foundation

skills.name: Python or MATLAB for engineering computation
MCNP or OpenMC (Monte Carlo radiation transport — basic)

certifications: 40-hour Radiation Worker Training (site-specific — provided by the lab)
OSHA 10-hour General Industry
applicationTips: INL is the US nuclear lab — the primary target for nuclear engineering students
LLNL and Sandia have significant nuclear security/weapons programs — require clearance eligibility
Mention any related coursework in the personal statement, even if incomplete
Nuclear engineering programs are small — a faculty connection helps
lliFreeResources: LLI Free Guide includes INL-specific pathway information
Michael Sarmiento (LLI ED) completed an INL internship — can speak to the experience

pathways.name: Computational Science & Data Analysis
pathways.description: Pathway for students with computing and data analysis skills pursuing research computing, scientific software engineering, or data science roles at national labs.

skills.name: Git version control (basic: commit, branch, push)
A domain science area (physics, chemistry, biology, etc.)

certifications: Google Data Analytics Certificate (free via Coursera — good starter)
AWS Cloud Practitioner (basic cloud familiarity)
Kaggle competitions (practical ML experience)
applicationTips: National labs are among the world's largest computing consumers — computing skills transfer widely
ANL (Aurora exascale), ORNL (Frontier), LBNL (NERSC) — all hire computational science interns
GitHub profile with real code is a strong differentiator — start building now
Domain pairing matters: CS + physics → HPC; CS + biology → bioinformatics; CS + materials → materials ML
lliFreeResources: LinkBot Newsletter regularly covers AI and computing research at national labs
LLI CDP provides personalized Python learning pathway recommendations

pathways.name: AI/ML Research at National Labs
pathways.description: Pathway for students pursuing AI, machine learning, and scientific computing research at DOE national labs, which are at the frontier of scientific AI (AuroraGPT, climate AI, drug discovery).
pathways.timeToReady: 12-18 months for students with programming baseline

skills.name: Python (advanced: classes, decorators, efficient data structures)
Scientific domain knowledge (chemistry, physics, biology, or climate)
Research paper reading and literature review skills

recommendedCourses: Machine Learning (Andrew Ng / Stanford CS229 or equivalent)
A domain science course (physics, chemistry, or biology)
certifications: fast.ai Practical Deep Learning for Coders (free)
DeepLearning.AI specializations (Coursera — often free audit)
Kaggle: Pandas, Machine Learning, Deep Learning certificates
applicationTips: National labs are building AI for science — not just consumer products
AuroraGPT (ANL), scientific ML for drug discovery (ORNL/LBNL), climate AI (PNNL) — each lab has unique AI research
Show applied projects: GitHub with ML projects is more valuable than just coursework
Scientific AI is different from industry AI — emphasize interest in domain applications
lliFreeResources: LinkBot Newsletter Issue 03: AI at National Labs — covers AuroraGPT, Frontier, PNNL grid security AI
LLI CDP provides curated AI learning pathways aligned to specific lab research areas

pathways.name: Materials Science & Engineering
pathways.description: Pathway for materials science, chemistry, and related engineering students pursuing characterization, synthesis, and computational materials research at national labs.

skills.name: Laboratory technique (wet lab, handling equipment safely)
Python or MATLAB for data analysis

recommendedCourses: Materials Science I (crystal structure, phase diagrams)
certifications: OSHA 40-hour HAZWOPER (for hazardous materials environments — some labs require)
Materials characterization workshops (many labs offer free online modules)
applicationTips: NIST SURF in Boulder or Gaithersburg is excellent for metrology and measurement science
Sean Florez (LLI co-founder) has a PhD in Materials Science from CU Boulder — strong mentoring for this pathway
ANL's Advanced Photon Source and ORNL's SNS accept students from diverse materials backgrounds
Perovskite solar cells, battery materials, advanced alloys — mention specific materials areas you're interested in
lliFreeResources: Sean Florez (LLI co-founder) patented materials science work — his career path is a model for this track
LinkBot Newsletter Issue 05: Synthetic Biology at National Labs — covers biomanufacturing and materials interfaces

pathways.name: Environmental Science & Ecology
pathways.description: Pathway for environmental science, ecology, and related students pursuing environmental monitoring, climate research, and ecological remediation work at national labs and federal agencies.
pathways.timeToReady: 6-12 months for students with biology/chemistry background

skills.name: Biology I (or Environmental Science foundation course)
GIS or spatial data basics (ArcGIS or QGIS)
Statistical analysis (R or Python for ecology)

certifications: OSHA 40-hour HAZWOPER (required for many environmental field positions)
Wilderness First Aid (helpful for field work)
applicationTips: PNNL and Savannah River NL have major environmental remediation programs
EPA GRO Fellowship is managed on Zintellect — apply alongside SULI/CCI
Hanford Site (PNNL) and INL have ongoing remediation work — mention interest in environmental remediation
Climate modeling roles are at LBNL, PNNL, ORNL — Python/R skills are essential for these
lliFreeResources: LLI internship page highlights EPA GRO alongside SULI/CCI
LinkBot Newsletter covers energy-environment interface topics

pathways.name: Mechanical Engineering
pathways.description: Pathway for mechanical engineering students pursuing design, simulation, manufacturing, and thermal systems work at national labs and defense contractors.

skills.name: MATLAB or Python for engineering computation

certifications: SolidWorks CSWA (Associate Certification — industry recognized)
OSHA 10-hour General Industry
applicationTips: ORNL's Manufacturing Demonstration Facility (MDF) is world-class — top ME internship target
NREL has rotating equipment, wind turbine dynamics, hydrogen storage systems — strong ME relevance
AFRL aerospace and propulsion divisions are premier ME destinations
Defense contractors (Lockheed, Northrop, Boeing) actively recruit ME interns with lab backgrounds
lliFreeResources: LinkBot Newsletter Issue 04: Robotics at National Labs — covers ORNL MDF and related ME applications

pathways.name: Electrical Engineering & Power Systems
pathways.description: Pathway for electrical engineering students pursuing power systems, grid modernization, RF systems, sensors, and electronics research at national labs.
pathways.timeToReady: 12-18 months with circuits and signals coursework

skills.name: MATLAB or Python for signal processing

certifications: National Instruments LabVIEW (useful for lab measurement)
MATLAB Onramp (free at MathWorks.com)
applicationTips: PNNL is the leading lab for grid modernization and energy storage EE work
Sandia has strong power electronics and microgrids programs
ANL Transportation Research Center — EV and charging infrastructure EE roles
AFRL has radar, RF, and defense electronics programs requiring EE backgrounds
lliFreeResources: LinkBot Newsletter Issue 02: Digital Twins — covers grid digital twins and power systems

pathways.name: Computer Science & Software Engineering
pathways.description: Pathway for computer science students pursuing scientific software engineering, HPC, cybersecurity, or systems research at national labs and industry.
pathways.timeToReady: 6-12 months for students with CS I under their belt

skills.name: Python (or Java/C++) — solid fundamentals (functions, classes, data structures)
Algorithms and data structures (lists, trees, graphs, sorting)
Debugging and testing (unit tests, print debugging)

recommendedCourses: CS I (Python or Java — any language)
certifications: Google IT Support Certificate (if systems oriented)
AWS Cloud Practitioner
GitHub Foundations Certificate
Kaggle Python Certificate
applicationTips: National labs write scientific software used worldwide — this isn't CRUD apps
ANL's Aurora, ORNL's Frontier, and LBNL's NERSC are world-class supercomputing environments
Cybersecurity roles at LLNL, INL, Sandia — national security computing
A GitHub profile with real projects (not just homework) is your strongest application asset
Community college CS students: mention specific projects, not just coursework
lliFreeResources: LinkBot Newsletter Issue 03: AI at National Labs — covers scientific computing roles
LLI CDP provides curated learning paths for CS students targeting specific labs

pathways.name: Biology & Life Sciences
pathways.description: Pathway for biology, biochemistry, and life science students pursuing molecular biology, bioinformatics, and bioenergy research at national labs and NIH.

skills.name: Biology I and II (cell, molecular, genetics)
Python or R for biological data analysis
Laboratory safety and hazardous material handling

certifications: NIH online training (several free modules at training.nih.gov)
Responsible Conduct of Research (required by NIH/NSF)
applicationTips: LBNL's Joint Genome Institute (JGI) is a world leader in microbial genomics
ORNL has computational biology and bioenergy programs
NIH SIP is the premier destination for pre-med and biology research students
Bioinformatics skills (Python/R) dramatically expand your options at national labs
lliFreeResources: LinkBot Newsletter Issue 05: Synthetic Biology at National Labs — covers JGI, Agile BioFoundry, ORNL biosciences

pathways.name: Business Analytics & Data
pathways.description: Pathway for business analytics and data-focused students pursuing roles in operations, finance, performance analysis, and program management at federal labs and clean energy organizations.

skills.name: Data visualization (Tableau, Power BI, or Python/matplotlib)
Statistics fundamentals (mean, median, regression, hypothesis tests)
Python or R basics for data analysis
Project management basics (Gantt charts, project scoping)
Process improvement awareness (Lean, Six Sigma concepts)

recommendedCourses: Introduction to Business or Principles of Management
certifications: Google Data Analytics Certificate (free via Coursera, widely recognized)
Tableau Public certification
Microsoft Excel Specialist
Project Management fundamentals (PMI or Google PM cert)
applicationTips: DOE Office of Science has business operations roles at labs and HQ (DOE Scholars program)
NREL has program analysts, finance, and operations roles for business students
Michael Sarmiento (LLI ED) has a Business Analytics background and went to INL + LLNL — proof this works
Federal agencies (EPA, DOE, NASA) have significant operations and analytics needs
Emphasize quantitative skills — SQL, Python, and Excel fluency are what separate applicants
lliFreeResources: Michael Sarmiento's career path (Business Analytics → INL → CARB → LLNL) is a direct model for this pathway
LLI CDP provides business-specific internship matching alongside STEM programs

pathways.name: Energy Policy & Public Affairs
pathways.description: Pathway for policy, political science, public administration, and communications students pursuing energy policy, regulatory affairs, and public engagement roles at federal agencies.
pathways.timeToReady: 3-9 months for students with writing and policy coursework

skills.name: Policy writing and analysis (briefing memos, regulatory summaries)
Energy systems fundamentals (electricity grid, oil & gas, renewables)
Environmental regulations (NEPA, Clean Air Act — awareness)

certifications: Google Project Management Certificate
Grant writing workshops (many free at community foundations)
applicationTips: DOE Scholars program specifically places policy and communications students at DOE offices
EPA has significant policy and regulatory affairs roles accessible to undergrads
Energy law and policy skills are increasingly valuable — grid, permitting, and climate regulation are expanding
Writing samples are critical for policy roles — compile 2-3 strong examples
lliFreeResources: LinkBot Newsletter grant pipeline section covers energy policy funding landscape
LLI grant pipeline work gives real exposure to how federal energy programs are funded and managed

pathways.name: Cybersecurity & Information Assurance
pathways.description: Pathway for cybersecurity students pursuing critical infrastructure protection, network security, and national security computing at DOE labs and federal agencies.
pathways.timeToReady: 6-12 months with networking and OS fundamentals

skills.name: Operating systems (Linux and Windows administration basics)
Risk assessment and security frameworks (NIST CSF basics)
Industrial control systems awareness (SCADA, ICS/OT — for national labs)
US citizenship (required for most national lab cybersecurity)

certifications: CompTIA Security+ (industry baseline — widely required for federal positions)
CompTIA Network+ (if networking is weak)
Google Cybersecurity Certificate (free via Coursera)
TryHackMe or Hack The Box (practical skills)
applicationTips: INL is the national leader in industrial control system (ICS/SCADA) cybersecurity — unique specialization
PNNL has grid cybersecurity programs — overlap with energy systems
LLNL and Sandia have significant national security computing / cyber programs
Security clearance eligibility (US citizenship + clean background) is often required
CTF participation shows practical skills — enter competitions and list them
lliFreeResources: INL is LLI's anchor lab partner — direct pathway to INL cybersecurity programs through our network

pathways.name: Robotics & Autonomous Systems
pathways.description: Pathway for robotics, autonomous systems, and mechatronics students pursuing unmanned systems, teleoperation, and autonomous inspection work at national labs and federal agencies.
pathways.timeToReady: 12-18 months with programming and physics foundation
certifications: ROS2 training (free online — The Construct or Udemy)
NVIDIA Isaac Sim (free certification for simulation)
applicationTips: INL operates robots in nuclear hot cells — one of the most demanding robotics environments in the world
ORNL's MDF has autonomous manufacturing research
PNNL deploys AUVs and remote systems for Hanford environmental characterization
NASA JPL has the most famous robotics program — extremely competitive but worth applying
Build a physical robot project — even a basic one on an Arduino shows hands-on capability
lliFreeResources: LinkBot Newsletter Issue 04: Robotics at National Labs — covers INL nuclear robots, ORNL MDF, PNNL AUVs, Sandia autonomy

pathways.name: Clean Energy Engineering
pathways.description: Pathway for students pursuing solar, wind, energy storage, hydrogen, and grid technology engineering careers at national labs and clean energy industry.

skills.name: Renewable energy systems overview (solar PV, wind, storage)
Energy systems analysis and efficiency metrics
MATLAB or Python for engineering analysis
Grid fundamentals (AC systems, power flow basics)

certifications: NABCEP PV Associate Exam (solar — entry level)
ACORE or Clean Energy States Alliance resources (free policy + tech courses)
applicationTips: NREL is the definitive clean energy research lab — the top target for this pathway
INL is building the world's first advanced nuclear microreactor — advanced energy, not just renewables
Business Analytics students: NREL and NextEra both have analytics roles for energy data
Tesla and NextEra Energy offer industry paths that complement lab research
lliFreeResources: LinkBot Newsletter Issue 01: Quantum Computing and its clean energy applications
LinkBot Newsletter Issue 02: Digital Twins at national labs (NREL wind/solar twins)

quickMatchQuestions.question: What is your primary academic focus?

options.label: Social Sciences / Policy (Political Science, Enviro Policy, Public Admin)

quickMatchQuestions.question: What type of school are you currently at?
What excites you most about a career at a national lab?

options.label: Doing cutting-edge research on a specific scientific topic
Computing, AI, or software that powers scientific discovery
Protecting national security and critical infrastructure
Solving the clean energy and climate challenge
Building, designing, and making things work
The business, operations, and policy that makes it all run

quickMatchQuestions.question: Where are you in your academic journey?

options.label: Sophomore or midway through CC program
priorityActions: Apply to CCI (CC students) or SULI (4-year)

options.label: Graduate student
priorityActions: Target NSF GRFP if not yet held
